mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows consistent growth over a 6-month period, but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which can lead to wage inflation and potential central bank tightening, ultimately pressuring corporate margins and equity valuations.
failure_condition: If UNRATE drops below 4.0% and remains there for at least 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of subsequent periods.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains relatively low, it implies an accommodative monetary policy that supports asset prices by keeping real interest rates low and encouraging borrowing and investment.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.2% MoM while FEDFUNDS is below 1.0% for 3 consecutive months, and the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of these periods.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rebound in housing starts (HOUST) following a downturn indicates renewed investment in the housing sector and broader economic recovery, signaling future demand and positive sentiment for equity markets.
failure_condition: If HOUST increases by more than 10% over any 3-month period during a recovery, but the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of subsequent periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing activity and robust supply-side capacity utilization, which underpins corporate profitability and drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows positive month-over-month growth for 6 consecutive months, but the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of these periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a weakening labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS declines by more than 0.5% MoM, but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of subsequent periods.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is rising aggressively, it indicates the central bank is tightening monetary policy to prevent overheating, which increases discount rates and can negatively impact equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5% in 3 months AND GDPC1 growth > 3% annualized
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.5% over 3 months while annualized GDPC1 growth exceeds 3%, but the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of these periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) signifies a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a strong fundamental backdrop for corporate earnings and, consequently, positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth for 4 consecutive quarters, but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of subsequent periods.
